questions.question: Which LinkedIn format is currently considered 'High Reach' and best for teaching complex frameworks slide-by-slide?

options.text: Text-only posts.
PDF Carousels.
LinkedIn Articles.
External links to your blog.

questions.explanation: Carousels allow you to break down information into digestible chunks, increasing 'dwell time' which LinkedIn's algorithm rewards.

questions.question: Why is 'White Space' and short paragraphs critical for text-only posts on LinkedIn?

options.text: Because LinkedIn charges more for extra words.
To avoid 'Walls of Text' that discourage users from reading on mobile devices.
Because the algorithm prefers shorter posts.
It isn't; long-form content is always better.

questions.explanation: Most LinkedIn users are on mobile. Scannability is key to keeping their attention as they scroll.

questions.question: When using the 'Proof' (P) pillar, what is often more effective than a professional stock photo?

options.text: A cartoon illustration.
An authentic, raw screenshot of a dashboard or result.
No image at all.
A photo of the founder's office.

questions.explanation: Authenticity builds trust. A raw screenshot demonstrates 'Practitioner Evidence' that generic stock photos cannot match.

questions.question: What is a mandatory requirement for video content on LinkedIn to ensure it is effective?

options.text: A Hollywood-style production level.
Subtitles (Captions), as most users watch with sound off.
Background music from a popular artist.
The video must be at least 10 minutes long.

questions.explanation: 80% or more of LinkedIn users watch video in 'Mute' mode. Without subtitles, your message is lost.

questions.question: In a poll, why should you almost always include an 'Other (Comment below)' option?

options.text: To trick the algorithm into thinking you have more comments.
To capture high-value, qualitative data and start direct conversations with engaged prospects.
Because LinkedIn's poll tool is broken and needs a fallback.
To make the poll look more professional.

questions.explanation: The poll is a conversation starter. The comments gathered from the 'Other' option provide deep insights into your ICP's real-world problems.

questions.question: Look at the PAIPS post you drafted earlier. Which specific format (Carousel, Text + Photo, Video, etc.) would best serve the 'Authority' and 'Proof' sections of that post? Why did you choose this format over the others for your specific ICP?
promptHints: Think about where your ICP spends time (Desktop vs Mobile).
Consider if your proof is visual (numbers/dashboards) or narrative (stories).
Evaluate your own comfort level and efficiency with different tools.
